blocks.content: After confessing to Miro and being rejected, Gaold became the subject of ridicule among the students.
Although he wasn't particularly laughable, students would burst into laughter whenever they saw him and offer mock words of comfort.
Gaold didn't care about their gazes.
Just as he had fully embraced the Yore faith, he didn't try to distort or rationalize his feelings.
What troubled him was seeing the woman he cherished the most hanging out with the person he disliked the most.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Look at them, sticking together again today.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Yeah, they couldn't stand each other at the beginning of the graduation class. Are they really dating, as the rumors say?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: What about Gaold? That's really sad.
Hearing the students' whispers, Gaold watched Miro and Sein walking together in the park.
Rumors were rampant in the graduation class that the two were dating.
Honestly, he didn't understand why Miro was hanging out with Sein.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: No, it would have been the same with anyone else.
Gaold turned away with a gloomy expression.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Damn it.
He didn't want to run into either Sein or Miro.
Paranormal Psychic Research Society?
One day, as the distance between them grew, Miro and Sein made a proposal.
Gaold didn't understand why they were starting a research society amidst their busy graduation schedules.
And a Paranormal Psychic Research Society at that?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: For someone like Gaold, who lived by the power of faith, it might make sense, but it didn't suit Sein at all.
Hmph, no principles? Giving up your beliefs for a girl?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Gaold was annoyed by everything.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: I'm not joining. Find someone else.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Why? I thought you'd like it. It's one of the reasons you're at the magic school.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: I don't know. I don't even want to talk to you.
As Gaold turned away, avoiding her gaze, Miro asked bluntly, "Don't tell me, you still have feelings for me?"

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Gaold couldn't say yes or no and just pouted his lips.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Alright then. If you don't want to, there's nothing I can do.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Gaold's heart raced.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: He didn't think he would care, but when she decided to withdraw, he felt he couldn't let it end like this.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Fine. I'll join the society.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: You should have said so from the start. Now we can finally collect membership fees!

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Wait, is that why you wanted me to join?

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Huh? No. Well, partly. Hehe.
Miro stuck out her tongue and turned to fill out the application.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: If I was going to do it, I should have just agreed from the beginning.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Gaold felt a wave of regret.
As a member of the society, Gaold's tasks were simple and laborious.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Damn it, why me…
He had to go around the magic warehouse Istas and record coordinates.
Sein used those coordinates to calculate something and discussed it with Miro.
Even in the society, Gaold always felt left out.
About a month later, bad rumors spread around the school.
It was said that Miro was breaking graduation rules and leaving the school every night.
Some students claimed to have seen her leaving in a carriage adorned with jewels that even the highest nobles couldn't afford.
The students concluded that Miro had found a benefactor.
What Miro was giving in return was obvious, even without saying it.
Of course, Gaold didn't believe it.
Miro was a genius and free-spirited.
She might hang out with rich old men for fun, but she wouldn't seek a benefactor.
However, the rumors grew like a snowball, and when Miro was degraded to a woman who sold her body, Gaold couldn't stand it and confronted Sein.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Hey, you! What's going on?

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: What?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Miro!

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Shouldn't we be doing something to stop these rumors? They're calling her a prostitute!

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: So what?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: What?
Gaold looked at Sein in disbelief.
Even at that moment, Gaold didn't realize how much inner conflict Miro was experiencing.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: You! How can you say that? Do you really care about Miro?

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Aren't you the one who doesn't trust Miro? Maybe deep down, you wish the rumors were true. Then you might have a chance.
Gaold clenched his fists and trembled.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Are you really dating Miro?

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: …Why should I tell you that?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: You bastard!
Gaold grabbed Sein by the collar and raised his fist.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: How low. Are you so blinded by jealousy that you've abandoned your beliefs?
The Yore faith opposed violence and followed love.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Gaold, who had believed in Yore all his life, couldn't bring himself to throw a punch.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Forget it. I won't associate with someone like you.
Gaold let go of Sein's collar and left the society.
A few days later, Gaold made a big decision.
He decided to follow Miro when she left the school after midnight.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Damn it. So it was true? Jealousy, my ass. Cold-hearted bastard.
A large house carriage was waiting where Miro was heading.
There weren't many people around at that hour, but it stood there proudly.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Hmph, not afraid of anything, are you?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Muttering sarcastically, Gaold followed the carriage.
Casting magic outside the school was forbidden, but considering the situation with Miro, it wasn't the time to worry about rules.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: He felt uneasy.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: The rumors might be true, or they might not.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: If they were true, what should he do?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: If they weren't, he would just be a petty man snooping on someone else's girlfriend.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Whatever!
Gaold decided to think about the consequences later and continued to follow the carriage.
The carriage stopped in a plain far from the school.
Thanks to the bright moon, he could see everything clearly.
A more luxurious carriage than the one Miro had arrived in was parked there, and an old man who looked nearly 100 years old got out.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Damn! He's a complete old geezer!
Gaold's disappointment in Miro doubled, but it was too early to judge.

blocks.speaker: Gustav Havitz XVI
blocks.content: Today is the promised last day. Have you made your decision?
Miro didn't answer.
A committee for the Judgment of the Twenty would soon be convened.
It was certain that Havitz would be one of the twenty.
The power of the three emperors would also have a strong influence within the committee.

blocks.speaker: Gustav Havitz XVI
blocks.content: You are truly beautiful. The most noble child in the world…

blocks.speaker: Gustav Havitz XVI
blocks.content: She could avoid being trapped in eternal loneliness, bearing the weight of humanity's fate.

blocks.speaker: Gustav Havitz XVI
blocks.content: But only if she became the beloved doll of Gustav Havitz.

blocks.speaker: Gustav Havitz XVI
blocks.content: Even so, it was something Miro had to consider.

blocks.speaker: Gustav Havitz XVI
blocks.content: Wouldn't it be better to be someone's doll than to bear the burden of all humanity?

blocks.speaker: Gustav Havitz XVI
blocks.content: From today, you are mine, Miro…
Havitz extended his snake-like tongue and approached Miro's neck.
He had no more desires left as the emperor of the empire, but Miro was different.
She was the most noble being in the world, the only hope beyond all of humanity.
Having her would be the perfect final amusement for someone who had ruled the world all his life.

blocks.speaker: Gustav Havitz XVI
blocks.content: You can live. You can continue to be happy. I will give you everything…
Even as the snake's breath approached, Miro just stared into the distance.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: You bastard!
As Havitz's lips touched her neck and he was about to bite her earlobe, a dull sound echoed, and his face turned.
With a groan, Havitz fell to the ground and looked up in disbelief.
A young man with an innocent face was glaring down at him.
Someone had struck the face of one of the three emperors.
No, more importantly, where were his guards?

blocks.speaker: Gustav Havitz XVI
blocks.content: You insolent fool! Do you know who I am?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Who cares? You're a perverted old man! And Miro, I'm really disappointed. No matter how much you like money and power, how could you be with someone like this…

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: What are you doing here?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: What do you mean? Of course, I…!
Gaold couldn't bring himself to be honest.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: The strange rumors were bothering me!
Havitz stood up and shouted.

blocks.speaker: Gustav Havitz XVI
blocks.content: This worthless human! What are you all doing? Cut off his head! No, capture him alive! I'll torture him for three years before killing him!
Havitz's voice echoed and then faded into silence.
Only then did the emperor realize something was wrong.
Miro's eyes had turned cold.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Let me make this clear.

blocks.speaker: Gustav Havitz XVI
blocks.content: Ugh…
Havitz's face turned pale as Miro suddenly seemed enormous.
It was the first time in his life he had felt such fear.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: I reject your offer. I am prepared to sacrifice myself for the future of humanity.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: The future of humanity? What future?
Miro ignored Gaold and turned away.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: And… it's best to keep today's events to yourself. Even an emperor can't handle everything.
Havitz finally understood why his guards hadn't responded.
From the moment she arrived, he had been trapped in Miro's space.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Let's go, Gaold.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Huh? Oh, okay.
Sensing the gravity of the situation, Gaold followed Miro.
After a brief silence, Miro left the space-time field and turned to Gaold.
The cold look from earlier was gone, and she laughed heartily, clutching her stomach.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: You're really something. Do you even know who you just hit?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Who cares? Who was it?

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: The emperor of the Gustav Empire, Havitz.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: What! The Gustav Empire!
Gaold, who had tried to maintain his pride, finally widened his eyes in shock.
Hitting an emperor?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: This wasn't just a matter of his family being wiped out; it could directly impact the safety of the kingdom.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: It's fine. He won't tell anyone.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: What on earth is going on? The future of humanity? What have you been doing?

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Before I explain, can I ask you something?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Sure.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: If you had known he was the emperor, would you still have done the same thing?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Of course. That filthy old man deserved it.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Miro didn't doubt Gaold's words.
From what she had seen in the extreme heat zone of the survival test, he was more than capable of such actions.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: But you used violence. Doesn't that go against the Yore faith?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Gaold finally realized and looked at his hands.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: It was the first time he had ever hit someone.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: No, before that, if he really was the emperor, how had he managed to get here without being caught?

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Did you know I was following you?
Miro didn't answer that and turned away.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Let's go back to school. I have something to tell you.
Even then, Gaold didn't know.
He didn't realize that what he would hear that night would change the next 20 years of his life completely.
